answer:
Fix, don't kill. LTV:CAC 2.2:1 is workable but thin (3:1 target); payback = $900/$150 = 6 mo, acceptable for B2B SaaS if churn holds.

Fix priorities:
1. Search terms report: kill non-converting keywords/queries, add negatives — typically cuts 20-30% waste.
2. Segment CAC by campaign/keyword. Kill anything >$1,200 CAC, shift budget to winners.
3. Bid on high-intent only (competitor, "pricing", "alternative to X" terms); pause broad/awareness.
4. Landing page CVR: even 20% lift → CAC ~$750.
5. Switch bidding to tCPA/tROAS w/ offline conversion import (closed-won, not just leads) — Google optimizes to wrong signal otherwise.
6. Check LTV by channel: Ads-sourced customers may churn faster/slower than blended $2k.

Kill only if: after 60-90 days of above, CAC stays >$1,000, or cash payback >6 mo strains runway.

$6k/mo ≈ 6-7 customers/mo — small sample, judge on 90-day windows not monthly.
